threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused when the bus driver shouts 'Next stop, get off!' like it’s a casual suggestion.
Last night on the 77, I saw a guy fold an entire pizza into his coat and casually walk off at Loughborough Junction. No one batted an eye.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a guy in full lederhosen playing accordion next to the Turkish bakery during the street festival. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find late-night U-Bahn trains running past abandoned tracks, with the occasional stray cat wandering for a midnight adventure.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by how we don’t flinch at the BART announcements; minor delays are just the background noise of our lives.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 31 on Mission; an older lady just broke out into song, completely unfazed by the bus lurching. That’s SF for you—chaos meets charm.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think La Rambla is the heart of Barcelona, but they miss the real spirit at Plaza del Sol, where locals gather and sip vermut.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell a newbie by their struggle with the 'horchata' pronunciation at the local cafés—it's like they’ve never had a drink before!

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think it's normal to avoid a 10-minute walk because it's 'too far'. Locals know everything’s just around the corner, even with a bus strike.
Last night on the 343, a guy serenaded the whole bus with a ukulele. Only in Peckham would you get a mini concert at 2 AM.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a guy at the Spreewaldplatz U-Bahn, juggling while simultaneously selling homemade falafel sliders – that's when it hit me. This place has a vibe.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you grab a beer from a Späti, then plop down on the curb to watch street artists make the pavement their canvas.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local when they instinctively know to avoid BART during rush hour—it's not just delays, it's a survival instinct.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy in a full tuxedo sprawled out on the Muni floor like it was a red carpet—no shame, just waiting for the next stop.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists think 'pintxos' are just bar snacks. In Gràcia, they’re a way of life—perfect for hopping between bars on a weekend evening.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot a recent arrival in Gràcia by their confusion over the vermut culture; they’ll order it without knowing it’s the aperitivo, not a full drink.
